plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-355 RP11.P06.M06.S04-RP11.P07.M03.S18 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-355 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP11.P06 permission phase (M06 synthetic fixture tail, M07 test/golden full cycle, M08 Hermes evidence and M09 Claude review 20-row cycles, M10 closeout 11-row head) and opens the RP11.P07 failure foundation (M00/M01 failure scope and contract heads, M02 type/shape 20-row failure cycle, M03 primary implementation 18-row head: failure taxonomy, missing tenant/actor/Matter/resource failures, unknown action, cross-tenant, permission denied, ambiguous rule, stale reference, lock conflict, retry exhaustion failures, rollback/compensation expectations, blocked-claim receipts, failure fixtures, failure tests, audit failure hints, Hermes failure evidence) across nine sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H11), Claude review packet (C11), and CP00-356 handoff, without opening rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-355 is a descriptor-only RP11 closeout pack (risk C, 150 units, RP11.P06.M06.S04-RP11.P07.M03.S18) closing the P06 permission phase tail and opening the P07 failure foundation. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and conservative: command-evidence.json asserts production_ready:false (status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout), no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false. The new CP355 slice in registry.js/service.js/validators.js follows the established pattern with every runtime/write/leak flag set false (freezeCp355Result service.js:1885), and validateTimeExpenseCoreCp355P06CloseoutP07FailureFoundationDescriptor (validators.js:2929) enforces descriptor-only rows, deny-over-allow, no cross-tenant access, no lock acquisition, no retry/rollback runtime, and no detail leakage. Coverage validation matches the prompt's deliverable distribution (impl 40, security_audit 26, ui 17, claude_review 4, test 14, failure_recovery 44, hermes_evidence 3, fixture 2 = 150) and phase counts (P06=90, P07=60). Tests (model.test.js:884-936) and the contract validator assert these boundaries substantively. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval/promotes_claude_to_final_approval/local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust all false, human final approval required, Claude packet read_only with only Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes patterns are evidence-packet/receipt descriptors only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. Review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:439) covers auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. Unrelated dirty files are correctly scoped out. One non-blocking P3 naming observation only. No P0/P1/P2 findings.

findings.title: Generator micro_title labels are semantically decoupled from row content
findings.details: required_section_micro_titles assigns rotating generic labels that do not describe the rows they group: RP11.P06.M06 is labeled 'Synthetic Fixture Set' but contains permission decision-binding rows (Mutation/Export/Share/AI retrieval decision binding, Allowed/Denied test); RP11.P07.M03 is labeled 'Primary Implementation Slice' but contains failure-recovery rows (Failure taxonomy, Missing tenant/actor/matter failures, etc.). The prompt's micro_title_distribution inherits these same labels. This is the weighted-implementation-ledger generator naming convention (source_ledger_sha pinned in review-baseline.json), is internally consistent, and is validated by exact-label equality in validateTimeExpenseCoreCp355Coverage, so it does not affect correctness, coverage, or boundary enforcement. It can mislead a human reader scanning micro titles for semantic intent.
findings.recommendation: No action required for closeout. Optionally, in a future generator revision, derive micro_title from the dominant deliverable/row semantics (or add a descriptive sub-label) so titles convey intent; otherwise document that micro_title is a generic positional label decoupled from row content.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as pre-existing, validated generation convention consistent across CP342-CP355; non-blocking and does not affect descriptor-only boundaries or coverage.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-355 time expense core p06 closeout p07 failure foundation descriptor pack
